name: Rotini with Eggplant-Pepper Sauce
recipeIngredient: 16 ounces plum tomatoes, canned with juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the eggplant in a medium bowl and sprinkle lightly with salt. Let stand for 15 minutes to absorb the salt and release excess moisture.
2. Rinse the eggplant well to remove the salt and pat dry with paper towels.
3. Bring white wine (or apple juice) or apple juice to a boil in a large no-stick frying pan. Add onions and simmer, stirring frequently, for 3 minutes, or until softened.
4. Add red, green, and yellow peppers, tomatoes (with juice), garlic, honey, and basil. Reduce heat to medium and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes, or until the peppers are very soft.
5. Add eggplant and simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce is thick.
6. Cook rotini in a large pot of boiling water until just tender. Drain well.
7. Return pasta to the pot and add sauce; toss well to combine.
15 minutes total preparation time. Per serving: 393 calories; 3 grams total fat (7%); 4 grams saturated fat.
EASY FREEZE: Make Ahead Weeknight Supper
Eggplant-Pepper Sauce tastes even richer after it's been frozen, so make extra for busy weeknights. Freeze cooked pasta in self-sealing plastic freezer bags and sauce in tightly lidded containers separately. Reheat sauce covered over low heat for 15 minutes or microwave on high power for 3 minutes.
